2604.26578.title: Graph Construction and Matching for Imperative Programs using Neural and Structural Methods
2604.26578.paper_summary: Reusing verification artefacts requires identifying structural and semantic similarities across programs and their specifications. In this paper, we focus on graph construction as a foundational step toward this goal. We present a pipeline that converts imperative programs and their annotations into typed, attributed graphs. Our experiments cover datasets including C with ACSL, Java with JML, and Dafny for C\#. The pipeline integrates abstract syntax tree parsing with semantic embeddings derived from models such as SentenceTransformer and CodeBERT. This enables the generation of graph representations that capture both structural relationships and semantic context. Our results show that consistent graph representations can be constructed across different languages and annotation styles. This work provides a practical basis for future steps in semantic enrichment and approximate graph matching for scalable verification artefact reuse.
2604.26578.paper_summary_zh: 重用驗證工件需要識別程序及其規範之間的結構和語義相似性。<lb/>在本文中，我們專注於圖形構建作為實現這一目標的基礎步驟。<lb/>我們提出了一個管道，將命令式程序及其註釋轉換為類型化的屬性圖。<lb/>我們的實驗涵蓋了包括 C 與 ACSL、Java 與 JML 以及 Dafny for C# 的數據集。<lb/>該管道將抽象語法樹解析與來自 SentenceTransformer 和 CodeBERT 等模型的語義嵌入相結合。<lb/>這使得生成的圖形表示能夠捕捉結構關係和語義上下文。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，可以在不同的語言和註釋風格之間構建一致的圖形表示。<lb/>這項工作為未來在語義豐富化和近似圖形匹配方面的可擴展驗證工件重用提供了實用基礎。
2604.26578.author: Arshad Beg et.al.
2604.26578.authors: Arshad Beg, Diarmuid O'Donoghue, Rosemary Monahan
